title: rubus chevalieri var. angkae
intro: Rubus chevalieri var. angkae, also known as Angkae raspberry, is a variety of the Rubus chevalieri plant, a species of flowering plant in the rose family. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, particularly in Indonesian and Malaysian folk medicine, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus chevalieri var. angkae include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating various gastrointestinal issues, as well as being used as an antiseptic and antidiarrheal agent. In herbal preparations, Rubus chevalieri var. angkae is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional remedies such as jamu, a type of Indonesian traditional medicine.
